item.title: Grameenphone appoints Andreas Høgberg as Chief Strategy and Transformation Officer
item.description: In this role, he will focus on driving innovation, setting strategic priorities, and steering organisational transformation to ensure sustainable growth and future readiness.

item.title: PM chairs meeting on DND dam to find ways to resolve waterlogging
item.description: He said the meeting discussed in detail the long-standing water-logging problem in the DND embankment area and possible ways to resolve it.

item.title: Bangladesh reach SAFF Women’s Championship Final for 3rd consecutive time
item.description: Bangladesh Women’s Football Team secured its third consecutive SAFF Women’s Championship Final.

item.title: Budget to be placed considering every citizen of Bangladesh: Finance Minister
item.description: The minister said that the government has been working on restoring sound public financial management to prevent the country from falling into a debt trap in the coming days, as a huge amount is now being spent to repay the interests of loans in the national budget.

item.title: Govt taking steps to fulfil election pledges: LGRD Minister
item.description: The minister said the government has taken initiatives to establish Thakurgaon University, Thakurgaon Medical College and an Engineering College, reopen the district airport and set up an Export Processing Zone (EPZ) to accelerate the area’s socio-economic development.

item.title: US ambassador meets water resources minister
item.description: The minister called for increased institutional investment in education, technological cooperation, vocational training, and research between the two countries.

item.title: Default loans rise by BDT 314.87 billion in first quarter of the year
item.description: Outstanding default loans in the banking sector exceeded approximately BDT 5.88 trillion at the end of March, accounting for 32.26 percent of total outstanding loans, according to Bangladesh Bank data.

item.title: Two Bangladeshis injured by BSF gunfire along Satkhira border
item.description: The two men had travelled to India “illegally” from Bangladesh about a year ago, according to police and local sources. They had since been working as construction labourers in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu after travelling through Howrah in Kolkata. They were reportedly attempting to return to Bangladesh illegally on the night of the incident.

item.title: Japan’s Mitsui delegation calls on PM
item.description: During the meeting, the two sides discussed investment opportunities and the expansion of investment in various sectors, including food, agriculture, energy and ICT.

item.title: Google must let UK publishers opt out of AI search under new rules
item.description: The country’s Competition and Markets Authority has flagged concerns about Google’s dominance in search, designating the company with ​the “strategic market status” that allows it to set targeted rules to increase trust and ​transparency.

item.title: US Proposes additional tariffs on goods from Bangladesh and 59 other economies
item.description: The proposal from the U.S. Trade Representative’s office is the latest finding from a Section 301 unfair trade practices investigation to be released as the Trump administration seeks to ​rebuild its emergency tariffs, which were struck down by a U.S. Supreme Court decision in ​February.

item.title: US sanctions Iran’s largest crypto exchange over IRGC links
item.description: The new sanctions follow a Reuters investigation published on May 1, which showed how Nobitex had become a central node in a parallel financial system used to process hundreds of millions of ​dollars for Iran’s central bank and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

item.title: Govt announces major legal reforms, heightened border security measures: Home Minister
item.description: The minister said a new law is being drafted to replace the antiquated 1867 Gambling Act, with plans to present it in the upcoming parliamentary session.

item.title: UN committee backs Bangladesh’s request for extension of LDC graduation period
item.description: CDP Chair Professor José Antonio Ocampo emphasised that Bangladesh would need to make significant progress in implementing key domestic reforms to address its existing structural vulnerabilities during this extended period.

item.title: Upcoming budget to focus on empowering poor, marginalised people: Finance Minister
item.description: The finance minister said the low-income people have historically been the most deprived in Bangladesh’s budgetary framework, adding that the government has therefore given priority to the poor, low-income groups, and homemakers (housewives) in the upcoming budget.

item.title: Operating expenditure accounted for 84% of government spending
item.description: A significant portion of the operating budget was spent on salaries and allowances for government employees, pensions, subsidies, and interest payments on loans.

item.title: Lebanon announces partial ceasefire between Israel, Hezbollah but attacks continue
item.description: According to Lebanon’s embassy in Washington, the agreement would not end the conflict in that country. But it calls for Israel to refrain from strikes on Beirut and its suburbs controlled by Hezbollah, while the Iran-aligned group would halt its attacks on Israel.

item.title: Nvidia has capacity to supply robust AI growth despite constraints, says CEO
item.description: The company, considered a barometer for the AI market’s health as its semiconductors are used in virtually every major data centre in the world, acknowledged, however, that supply constraints remain a ​concern.

item.title: STMicroelectronics raises data centre revenue target on AI demand
item.description: The Franco-Italian chipmaker now ​expects data centre revenue of about $1 ​billion in 2026, compared with its previous forecast for revenue “nicely above” $500 million.

item.title: Gold price falls by BDT 3,266 per bhori
item.description: As in previous adjustments, the association said in the statement that the price of pure gold in the local market has declined. Considering the overall market situation, new gold prices have therefore been fixed.

item.title: Russian drones, missiles strike Ukraine in major attack; 11 dead, scores wounded
item.description: Russia has targeted Ukraine’s power supply and infrastructure in a war now more than four years old, while Ukraine has stepped up attacks this year on Russian oil facilities. Both deny targeting civilians.

item.title: Remittance inflow rises 15.34% to $3.43 billion in May
item.description: The country received $3.43 billion in remittances during May 2026 against $2.97 billion in May 2025, reflecting the continued confidence of expatriate Bangladeshis in using formal banking channels to send money home.

item.title: Tofail Ahmed is no more
item.description: In a statement, the hospital authorities said that the politician had long been suffering from various age-related complications. On September 24, he was admitted to Square Hospital due to pneumonia-related respiratory distress, heart disease, and physical weakness. He had been on life support there.

item.title: Entrepreneurs and businesses concerned over NBR’s tax plans
item.description: Entrepreneurs and businesses fear that the government may ultimately seek to address the substantial revenue shortfall by placing additional burdens on existing taxpayers instead of broadening the tax base by bringing new taxpayers into the system.

item.title: CHT Affairs Minister Dipen Dewan resigns
item.description: Dipen Dewan cited physical illness as the reason for his resignation, according to sources from the Prime Minister’s Press Wing.

item.title: Trial begins in Ramisa murder case, testimony hearing set for tomorrow
item.description: The two accused were brought to court in a prison van at around 7:45 am. Shortly before 11:00 am, Sohel Rana was produced before the court under tight police security. About ten minutes later, Swapna Akter was also brought before the tribunal.

item.title: China tightens overseas investment rules after blocking Meta-Manus deal
item.description: The regulations provide ​for the first time a comprehensive and formalised legal basis for China to force the unwinding of completed overseas ​transactions — heightening compliance risks for global investors in sensitive sectors like Chinese tech and AI.

item.title: Oil rises as US and Iran trade strikes, Israel moves further into Lebanon
item.description: The stepped-up fighting, coming just after the U.S. hosted Israel-Lebanon peace talks in Washington ​on Friday, dimmed expectations that the U.S. and Iran could soon announce an extension to their ceasefire agreement, which ​had driven Brent and WTI to settle down 1.8 percent and 1.7 percent, respectively, on Friday.

item.title: US says it struck Iranian military sites, Tehran responds with air base attack
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have sporadically exchanged strikes since their ceasefire took effect in early April as diplomacy aimed at a more durable agreement drags on.

item.title: State to press for maximum punishment in Ramisa case: Law Minister
item.description: The law minister stated that the government expect that the available evidence will ensure the maximum punishment for the accused.

item.title: Govt committed to fulfilling all public expectations: PM
item.description: The prime minister said that the current government is running the state, being inspired by the philosophy of Shaheed President Ziaur Rahman, and the main goal of the government is to fulfil the hopes and aspirations of the people through implementation of the election manifesto.

item.title: Netanyahu orders deeper Israeli incursion into Lebanon to hit Hezbollah
item.description: In the latest advance, Israeli troops seized the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle and a ​strategic ridge in southern Lebanon, the military said.

item.title: Malta’s Labour Party wins record fourth term in parliamentary election
item.description: The Maltese prime minister stated that this is a ‌victory of all the people based on the programme the government presented to all the people.

item.title: Eid holiday ends, people begin returning to the capital
item.description: The presence of travellers consequently increased at the capital’s main entry points.

item.title: Terrorist hideouts will be completely eliminated wherever they exist: Home Minister
item.description: The home minister said that the government’s objective is to fully bring the law and order situation in areas such as Salimpur, Jungle Salimpur, Alinagar, and surrounding regions under control and establish lasting peace.

item.title: BCIC turns to direct purchase of Russian urea as tenders fail to attract expected response
item.description: The state-owned agency has already submitted a proposal to the government procurement committee to import 100,000 tonnes of fertiliser from Russia.

item.title: Eid holiday brings tourist rush to Sajek and Rangamati
item.description: Tourists from different parts of the country are exploring the district’s popular attractions, while many are taking engine-powered boats to tour Kaptai Lake.

item.title: Over 500,000 rawhides arrive at Savar tannery estate
item.description: A large portion of the hides arriving earlier in the day were fresh, blood-stained raw hides. After reaching the tanneries, workers quickly applied salt to preserve them.

item.title: India invokes Ziaur Rahman’s 1971 radio address in tribute on martyrdom anniversary
item.description: In a statement issued to mark the occasion, the Indian High Commission described Ziaur Rahman as one of Bangladesh’s bravest sons.

item.title: PM urges nation-building inspired by Ziaur Rahman’s ideals
item.description: “Take an oath to dedicate yourself to nation-building, inspired by the ideals of Shaheed Zia,” Tarique Rahman said.

item.title: Zia laid modern Bangladesh’s economic base thru politics of dev, production: Moyeen Khan
item.description: Dr Abdul Moyeen Khan made these remarks in an interview with BSS on the eve of the 45th martyrdom anniversary of Ziaur Rahman, observed on May 30.

item.title: Rain-soaked Dhaka fears dengue cases may soon rise
item.description: Public health specialists note that this year’s weather conditions have sharpened the threat. Repeated rainfall interspersed with warmer spells has sped up mosquito breeding.

item.title: Hegseth tones down warnings about China but says US remains committed to Pacific security
item.description: Hegseth, who was with Trump in Beijing, said the two leaders had agreed that China and the U.S. should “build a constructive relationship of strategic stability.”

item.title: Cattle sellers count losses as Eid supply outstrips demand
item.description: Inflation-hit households cut back on qurbani, leaving many sellers facing losses after a last-minute price slump.

item.title: US commander meets with Cuban military officials as Trump pressures island nation
item.description: Cuba’s Revolutionary Armed Forces said in a statement that both sides viewed “the meeting positively because it addressed security issues along the perimeter separating the military enclave, and they agreed to maintain communication between the two military commands.”

item.title: Myanmar’s junta chief turned president heads to India, with an eye on China
item.description: The five-day trip, during which the former general will hold talks with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, underscores the gradual return of regional re-engagement for Myanmar.

item.title: 45th death anniversary of former president Ziaur Rahman today
item.description: To mark the anniversary, BNP has announced an eight-day programme from May 25 to June 1.

item.title: Ziaur Rahman’s ideals will continue to inspire new generation: President
item.description: The president said this in a message issued on the eve of the 45th martyrdom anniversary of former President Ziaur Rahman.

item.title: Iran’s strongest card in nuclear talks: its highly enriched uranium
item.description: While much of Iran’s uranium enrichment infrastructure was destroyed or badly damaged when Israel and the U.S. bombed ​it in June, a large part of the highly enriched uranium it amassed is thought to have survived.

item.title: Trump says he will soon decide on Iran deal, demands reopening of Hormuz Strait
item.description: Trump is under pressure to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and get U.S. gasoline prices down ahead of the November congressional elections, as voters show increasing frustration over rising prices.

item.title: Unsold rawhide dumped on streets in Brahmanbaria
item.description: After waiting through the night without finding customers, many dragged unsold hides to other parts of town before dumping them.

item.title: Ten child deaths push Bangladesh measles outbreak toll to 575
item.description: Since the outbreak began on March 15, the country has registered 90 confirmed measles deaths and 485 suspected measles deaths.

item.title: Oil tanker wagon derails in Moulvibazar, severing Sylhet rail link
item.description: The derailment occurred around 3:30 pm on the Bhanugach–Sreemangal section as the Sylhet-to-Akhaura train passed through the forest.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launchpad in a setback for bid to catch Musk’s SpaceX
item.description: Blue Origin ​was preparing the rocket for its fourth launch, which was due to deliver 48 Amazon Leo satellites into low-Earth orbit, part of efforts ​to build a broadband constellation to rival Musk’s Starlink network.

item.title: Sudanese medical group accuses paramilitary force of killing 27 in attack targeting civilians
item.description: Sudan Doctors Network, a group that tracks violence across the country, blamed forces affiliated with the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces for carrying out the attacks.

item.title: With a stalemate in Ukraine and discontent at home, Putin seems ready to escalate his war
item.description: Russia’s warning to carry out “consistent and systematic” missile strikes on Kyiv, accompanied by a call for evacuating foreign embassies from the capital, signals Putin’s intention to expand Russia’s barrage despite the heavy costs and potential international outrage.

item.title: Moderately heavy rain likely in parts of Bangladesh
item.description: The extended portion of a western low-pressure system stretching from West Bengal to the north Bay of Bengal is driving the conditions, the met office said.

item.title: Bangladeshi migrant killed in Iraq missile strike repatriated to Dhaka
item.description: The state minister for foreign affairs received the remains at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport at 4:45 am.

item.title: Ward closed after DGHS finds infrastructure flaws: Dr Zahid Raihan
item.description: The six deaths occurred in the Ad-din Hospital’s post-operative ward on Wednesday morning, triggering a probe and a police case.

item.title: Merit will decide Thakurgaon University appointments, says minister
item.description: The minister said no opportunity for nepotism would be created in the appointment of faculty or other staff.

item.title: Natore hide traders fear losses amid low supply, labour shortage
item.description: A shortage of workers to salt and preserve raw hides left part of the collected stock lying on the road outside the Chak Baidyanath market.

item.title: Rawhide prices fall despite higher government rates
item.description: Seasonal traders and sellers at the main tannery hub in Savar reported receiving BDT 600 to 700 for the best quality raw cowhides on Thursday, BDT 150 to 200 a piece lower than in the previous year

item.title: Oil falls over 1 percent on reports of potential US-Iran ceasefire deal
item.description: Prices have been volatile in recent ‌sessions, ⁠swinging by as much as $6 for both benchmarks on conflicting signals over a possible end to the three-month U.S.-Israeli war on Iran and the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Iran, US reach deal to extend ceasefire, pending Trump’s approval
item.description: News of the possible agreement came after a round of tit-for-tat attacks between the two ​countries, the latest such incident since the ceasefire took effect in early April.

item.title: Measles outbreak death toll reaches 565
item.description: No deaths from confirmed measles infection were recorded in the 24-hour period to 8 am Thursday, the directorate said in a statement.

item.title: China works on AI token futures market, sources say, in race with US
item.description: The Shanghai Futures Exchange is in the early stages of designing futures contracts for so-called AI tokens, said one of the people familiar with the matter.

item.title: From diapers to drugs: How India’s global corporate hubs are putting AI to work
item.description: Heads of several Global Capability Centres (GCCs) told Reuters ‌they are deploying AI across a host of functions - from marketing and content creation to finance and human resources.

item.title: Govt working for unity, nation-building: Home Minister
item.description: The minister said all citizens, regardless of political affiliation, must unite for the country’s development under the slogan, “Bangladesh First.”

item.title: Dhaka South targets clearing 15,935 tonnes of sacrificial waste in eight hours
item.description: The DSCC administrator said he expected all of the first day’s waste to be shifted to the landfill within eight hours of the start of operations

item.title: President calls for building prosperous Bangladesh in spirit of Eid-ul-Adha
item.description: In a video message recorded at Bangabhaban on the morning of Eid, the President conveyed his heartfelt greetings and ‘Eid Mubarak’ to the countrymen as well as the Muslim community across the world.

item.title: LGRD minister calls for unity after Eid prayers in Thakurgaon
item.description: The minister made the remarks at his residence in Thakurgaon after attending prayers at the town’s central Eidgah ground.

item.title: Madrasah students and traders fan out for hide collection on Eid day
item.description: The annual trade is an important source of raw material for Bangladesh’s leather industry but is perennially vulnerable to quality loss from improper curing and transport delays.

item.title: PM offers prayers at graves of army officers
item.description: The premier, along with his wife Dr Zubaida Rahman, arrived at the Banani Military Graveyard around 9:15 am.

item.title: PM visits parents’ graves, offers prayer
item.description: After attending Eid prayers at the National Eidgah, the prime minister and his wife Zubaida Rahman arrived at the gravesite of his parents at Sher-e-Bangla Nagar in the city.

item.title: Raw hide demand climbs as Savar tanneries target bigger Eid haul
item.description: Tannery owners there expect to buy 1 million to 2 million more pieces in the first month after Eid-ul-Adha this year than they did in 2025.

item.title: Oil tanker catches fire at Chattogram outer anchorage; 22 crew rescued
item.description: The crew were brought to safety and no casualties or missing persons had been reported.

item.title: ‘Breakneck’ Ebola epidemic in Congo outpaces world’s response
item.description: Caused by a ‌strain of the virus known as Bundibugyo for which there is no vaccine or treatment, the outbreak has already caused a suspected 220 deaths and 900 cases, according to the WHO.

item.title: Oil surges after Iran targets US airbase in retaliation
item.description: The ‌U.S. ⁠military launched new strikes in Iran targeting a military site that officials believed posed a threat to U.S. forces and commercial maritime traffic in the strait, a U.S. official told Reuters.

item.title: India’s PM extends Eid greetings to PM Tarique Rahman
item.description: The Indian prime minister said the festival embodies the timeless ideals of sacrifice, compassion and brotherhood, which are essential for fostering a peaceful and inclusive world.

item.title: Iran and US trade air strikes after Trump dismisses report of Hormuz deal
item.description: The escalation in hostilities highlighted threats to the tenuous ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran that took effect in early April, dampening hopes for a peace deal and sending oil prices surging again.

item.title: Bangladesh celebrates Eid-ul-Adha with prayers for peace, prosperity
item.description: Favourable weather, after days of rain in the capital, encouraged crowds to gather from early morning, many accompanied by family and friends.

item.title: Eid-ul-Adha congregation held at Parliament South Plaza
item.description: The congregation, held at 7:30 am local time, included Parliament Secretary Barrister Md Golam Sorwar Bhuiyan, other officials and members of the public.

item.title: President, PM offer Eid-ul-Adha prayers at National Eidgah
item.description: After the namaz, they joined a munajat with the devotees, seeking blessings from the Almighty for the Muslim Ummah and the nation.

item.title: Ten players to watch at the FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: From Norwegian goal machine Erling Haaland to Team USA’s Christian Pulisic, here are 10 players to watch during this year’s World Cup.

item.title: Trump gathers Cabinet as he looks to seal deal to end war that some backers worry will embolden Iran
item.description: As he prepares to huddle with his top aides, Trump is projecting confidence that he’s closing in on a deal that will reopen the Strait of Hormuz and provide him a credible argument that Iran’s nuclear capability has been diminished enough to declare victory, winding down a conflict that’s been politically unpopular for Republicans.

item.title: Ward where six newborns died lacked ventilation when AC was off, says Health DG
item.description: Director General Professor Pravath Chandra Biswas told reporters after inspecting Ad-Din Medical College Hospital in Dhaka’s Mogbazar area that the room had been left with no alternative air supply.

item.title: Qurbani waste to be removed within 6-8 hours: RCC administrator
item.description: In accordance with the Prime Minister’s directive, the city corporation will remove sacrificial animal waste and clean the city within 6 to 8 hours, said the Rajshahi City Corporation’s administrator.

item.title: Bandarban tourism slows before Eid-ul-Adha
item.description: The sluggish pace came even as the district administration waived entry fees to four popular tourist spots for two days to draw visitors.

item.title: Four dead after bus overturns, hits oncoming vehicle in Dhaka’s Nadda
item.description: The Islam Paribahan coach was travelling from Patuakhali to Bimanbandar-Uttara and passing the Nadda New Bazar area at high speed when the driver lost control, police and witnesses said.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah clash along a strategic Lebanese river after overnight strikes
item.description: A U.S.-brokered ceasefire in the Israel-Hezbollah conflict appeared more nominal by the day, complicating efforts at a broader peace in the Iran war as Tehran wants an agreement to include an end to the fighting in Lebanon.

item.title: Flooded lanes and stranded traffic deepen the capital’s monsoon misery
item.description: Experts blame the absence of an adequate drainage system, with encroached canals and waterbodies, unplanned urbanisation and a feeble drainage network compounding the crisis.

item.title: Six newborns die at Dhaka’s Ad-Din hospital; relatives blame gas leak
item.description: The infants, aged between one and two days, were all in a post-operative ward, hospital authorities said.

item.title: PM issues Eid-ul-Adha greeting ahead of nationwide celebration
item.description: In a message posted to his verified Facebook page, the prime minister described the occasion as carrying “an eternal message of deep faith in the Creator, self-surrender and sacrifice”.

item.title: Pentagon spars with SpaceX over Starlink price hike during Iran war
item.description: The Pentagon has also been at odds with SpaceX over pricing for a plan to provide the populace direct-to-cell connections with Starlink akin to 5G service, two of the sources said.

item.title: Iran says new US strikes violate ceasefire
item.description: Iran's foreign ministry said U.S. strikes in Iran's southern Hormozgan province, where Iranian media reported sounds of explosions early on Tuesday, represented a "gross violation" of a tenuous ceasefire in place for nearly seven weeks.

item.title: Third Bangladeshi woman summits Mount Everest after 14-year gap
item.description: Her climb follows the ascents of Nishat Mazumder on May 19, 2012 and Wasfia Nazreen on May 26, 2012, the first two Bangladeshi women to conquer Everest.

item.title: Trump links Abraham Accords to any Iran deal
item.description: Longtime Trump ally Senator Lindsey Graham embraced the idea of linking Iran to the Abraham Accords as something that would lead to regional integration and create “a powerhouse for ​economic opportunity.”

item.title: Ten more children die in measles outbreak, toll hits 555
item.description: Since March 15, a total of 88 people have died from confirmed measles and 467 from suspected measles.

item.title: Rain hits Dhaka livestock markets during peak Eid trading
item.description: A downpour that began around midday in Dhaka and lasted up to two hours left market grounds waterlogged, forcing a large share of buyers to return empty-handed and leaving traders struggling to shelter their animals.

item.title: Minister says Eid animal trade now key income source for marginal farmers
item.description: Farmers and livestock rearers raise cattle, goats, sheep and buffalo for one to two years before bringing them to market for Eid-ul-Adha, creating a critical economic window for smallholders, the livestock minister said.

item.title: DSCC ready to remove sacrificial waste within eight hours
item.description: Around 174,406 animals are expected to be sacrificed within DSCC areas this year.

item.title: Power Division assures uninterrupted electricity supply during Eid
item.description: The Power Division has said it has ensured round-the-clock monitoring of power generation, transmission and distribution systems, while emergency repair teams have been kept on standby to respond quickly to any disruption.

item.title: Khulna’s Sheikhpara traders forced into open-air hide market
item.description: The area, known locally as Sheikhpara Chamrapatti, once housed about 10 shops and 35 traders. Most of those shops have now closed.

item.title: Eid-ul-Adha congregation at Parliament Building South Plaza at 7:30 am
item.description: All concerned have been requested to arrive on time to attend the Eid congregation.

item.title: Bogura hide traders head into Eid season with BDT 220 million unpaid dues
item.description: More than 200 traders routinely borrow ahead of the festival to purchase hides and salt, relying on full payment from Dhaka-based tanneries to recover their capital.

item.title: Ready-made tools blunt demand for Mirsarai’s traditional blacksmiths
item.description: In the old blacksmith quarters of markets such as Mithachhara Bazar, the ring of hammer on anvil still rises with the festival season, but craftsmen report that rising iron and coal prices have cut their margins while customers increasingly shun bespoke work.

item.title: BDT 250 billion highway upgrade fails to ease Eid gridlock on northern route
item.description: Experts blame the protracted upgrade works as a principal cause of congestion in the Dhaka–Tangail artery. Sprawling roadside markets and mismanagement only compound the gridlock.

item.title: Israel’s military says it’s striking Hezbollah sites as Netanyahu vows to ‘increase the blows’
item.description: Netanyahu’s warning came as Hezbollah has been firing fiber optic drones at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon and northern Israel in recent weeks.

item.title: Brent oil gains 2 percent as US military strikes on Iran add to uncertainty on potential peace deal
item.description: Brent futures were up $1.98, or 2.1 percent, to $98.12 a barrel as of 0405 GMT, after settling 7 percent lower in the previous session.

item.title: Rubio says Iran deal could take days as US launches fresh strikes
item.description: Describing the strikes against targets including boats attempting to lay mines ‌and missile launch sites, Rubio said the Strait of Hormuz has to be open “one way or the other.”

item.title: Experts discuss opportunities, risks in evolving US–Bangladesh trade ties
item.description: The discussion examined the implications of the recent US–Bangladesh trade agreement, its possible effects on major sectors of the Bangladeshi economy, and the broader strategic, regulatory, and geopolitical questions surrounding the agreement.

item.title: Fire breaks out at Kalshi slum in capital, four Fire Service units deployed
item.description: Three additional units from the Mirpur and Kurmitola fire stations are also being sent to assist.

item.title: Trump and Warsh’s fates are now tied, for better or worse
item.description: With new Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh now installed as the country’s central ​bank chief — completing Trump’s imprimatur across the top echelon of U.S. economic policymaking posts — the dynamic shifts.

item.title: Rethinking fiscal governance: A blueprint for Bangladesh’s tax future
item.description: Fiscal reform in Bangladesh must go beyond revenue targets. It is not enough to chase numbers; the system must be rebuilt to foster trust and voluntary compliance. By embracing transparency, democracy, prestige, agility, and empathy, the country can unlock a new wave of civic participation.

item.title: Indian finance minister calls for focus on ‘3Fs’ – fuel, fertiliser, forex
item.description: The prime minister ​giving a call to conserve foreign exchange, as far ​as possible, is very important, Sitharaman said, adding that the stress on 3Fs – fuel, fertiliser, and foreign exchange – should be viewed ​in this context.

item.title: Raw cotton imports through Bhomra Land Port fall by nearly 40%
item.description: Traders and importers said imports have decreased because of lower demand from local garment factories and textile mills producing yarn.

item.title: ADB unveils $5 billion financing for Bangladesh over five years amid cost pressures
item.description: During a visit to Dhaka, ADB President Masato Kanda met the prime minister, finance minister, and private sector leaders, pledging expanded support for reforms, financing gaps, energy diversification and private investment mobilisation.

item.title: Measles and its symptoms claim 17 more lives in 24hrs
item.description: DGHS on Monday disclosed the information in the measles-related report.

item.title: President to host Eid-ul-Adha reception at Bangabhaban
item.description: The president and his spouse will exchange greetings with distinguished personalities and people from various classes and professions.

item.title: ADB President calls on PM
item.description: The meeting was held at the Prime Minister’s Office in Tejgaon in the capital this morning.

item.title: Bangladesh farmland faces twin threat of acidity and depleted soil quality
item.description: Soil scientists say that an increase in soil acidity directly affects agricultural production. In acidic soil, normal root growth is hindered, and plants cannot properly absorb essential nutrients, they added.

item.title: RAB camp in Jungle Salimpur attacked; infrastructure demolished with bulldozers
item.description: During the attack, bulldozers were used to demolish the camp’s walls and other infrastructure. Roads were also dug up at least three locations in an attempt to obstruct operations by law enforcement agencies.

item.title: Defaults deter banks from fresh lending to leather sector
item.description: The livestock ministry projects that more than 10 million cattle, buffaloes, goats and sheep will be sacrificed. The loan target for preserving these hides is just BDT 2.28 billion, but industry insiders warn actual disbursements could slip below BDT 1 billion, as they did last year.

item.title: Trump says not to rush as details emerge of a potential Iran deal
item.description: Trump said negotiations were “proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner,” and the relationship with Iran was becoming “much more professional and productive.”

item.title: DNCC deploys 16,000 workers for Eid waste management
item.description: According to DNCC, around 6,500 sanitation workers, 4,500 household waste collectors and nearly 5,500 volunteers will work in the field to ensure swift removal of sacrificial waste from Eid day.

item.title: BAJUS lifts gold, silver prices as domestic pure gold costs climb
item.description: The BAJUS Standing Committee on Pricing and Price Monitoring set the new rates at a meeting on Monday morning.

item.title: Bangladesh offers export rights, tax breaks in offshore energy push
item.description: The move follows a failed 2024 offshore round in which no bids were submitted after investors raised concerns over pricing, tariffs, data availability and labour-related costs. Those issues were reviewed over the past year, leading to a redesigned contract framework and updated terms

item.title: At least 15 dead as truck overturns near Jamuna Bridge
item.description: The vehicle, bound for northern Bangladesh from Dhaka, lost control and flipped on the highway, killing 15 at the scene.

item.title: China sends astronaut on year-long space mission as it eyes 2030 moon landing
item.description: The Shenzhou-23 flight will execute the first autonomous rapid rendezvous and docking procedure with the core module of Tiangong in preparation for the 2030 mission.

item.title: Vessels carrying Middle East oil, LNG exit Hormuz, head for Pakistan, China
item.description: The vessels ​are among a handful of supertankers exiting the Gulf this month via a transit route ⁠that Iran has ordered ships to use.

item.title: Oil slips 6% as US, Iran seen moving closer to peace deal
item.description: Brent crude futures fell $5.85, or 5.7 percent, to $97.69 a barrel, while U.S. West ​Texas Intermediate were at $90.85 a barrel, down $5.75, or 6 percent.

item.title: Central bank announces BDT600B stimulus package to reopen factories, create jobs, revive economy
item.description: The package, titled “Private Sector Development, Employment Generation and Economic Revival,” aims primarily to revitalise the country’s economy. It emphasises reopening closed industrial units, creating new employment opportunities, strengthening the agriculture and export sectors, and increasing the flow of funds into the private sector to restore momentum in overall economic activities.

item.title: On first official India trip, Rubio tries to halt a trust deficit between Washington and Delhi
item.description: Rubio arrived Saturday on his first official visit to India ahead of a meeting set for Tuesday with his counterparts from India, Australia and Japan, which are members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance known as the Quad.

item.title: Beyond relief: rethinking the Rohingya response
item.description: The Rohingya response is no longer only a humanitarian challenge. It is also a question of long-term policy, regional stability, and shared responsibility. Continuing to meet immediate needs remains essential, but it must be complemented by forward-looking approaches that expand opportunities and reduce dependency over time.

item.title: Why democracy so often slips away after an uprising and what it takes to hold on
item.description: Democracy is not born in a single moment of uprising. It is built or lost in what comes after.

item.title: Dhaka, Delhi divided over water-sharing basis as Ganges treaty nears expiry
item.description: Neither government has yet said whether the existing pact will be renewed or replaced, but their respective ministries confirm that technical teams from both sides have begun preliminary work.

item.title: Thomas Dooley appointed new head coach of Bangladesh national football team
item.description: BFF is hopeful and thrilled to appoint American-origin German Thomas Dooley as the new head coach of the Bangladesh national football team.

item.title: Korea Post eyes AI data centres, real estate investment for higher returns amid mail losses
item.description: Korea Post is still cautious on office ​buildings, but is looking at so-called secondary funds investing in data centres, logistics facilities, and multi-family houses in North America and ​Europe.

item.title: Samsung’s deal with union hailed as a victory as bonuses less generous than SK Hynix’s
item.description: Samsung workers had been furious about the large gap in bonuses with their peers at smaller rival SK Hynix, which had beaten Samsung to the punch ​in delivering advanced AI chip units to Nvidia, bolstering its fortunes.

item.title: Ammonium, phosphate levels in Karnaphuli several times higher than normal
item.description: The critical condition poses a serious threat to aquatic life, fish breeding, and the overall ecological balance of the river.

item.title: The worst climate future is less likely, but the best one is slipping away, scientists say
item.description: Researchers’ new list of seven plausible carbon pollution scenarios for the future are pushing aside two staples of climate policy: the extremes on either end.

item.title: SpaceX IPO filing lays bare losses and Musk control as it stakes future on AI
item.description: For many, Musk’s record turning Tesla into the most ‌valuable auto company in the world and developing the world’s first fully reusable rocket and largest satellite network is enough to justify investment.

item.title: SpaceX’s upgraded Starship V3 ready for debut launch ahead of IPO
item.description: The towering spacecraft, ⁠consisting of the upper-stage Starship astronaut vessel stacked atop its Super Heavy booster rocket, was due for launch as early as Thursday, the company said late on Monday.

item.title: At least 18 killed as heavy rains drench southern, central China
item.description: China’s weather agency said areas of Jiangxi, Anhui, Hunan, Hubei, Guizhou, Guangxi, Guangdong and Hainan faced a ​high risk of rain-related disasters, including landslides, flash floods and severe urban flooding, and waterlogging.

item.title: ‘Actuary’ in insurance industry and its importance, current status in Bangladesh
item.description: Actuarial science is a highly respected profession and offers both prestige and substantial financial rewards. Unfortunately, in Bangladesh, the actuarial profession has not yet developed adequately.

item.title: Bangladesh secure ‘hat-trick’ Test win over Pakistan
item.description: With the victory, the side led by Najmul Hossain Shanto took a 1-0 lead in the two-match Test series.

item.title: “A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation”
item.description: “The fundamental weakness is that we still treat the budget essentially as an income-expenditure ledger. A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation. It should spell out which sectors the government will prioritise over the coming year…”

item.title: “Upcoming budget will include every individual and community”
item.description: “In the past, many groups remained outside the scope of the budget, with no dedicated projects for them. This time, we will bring them under inclusion. We have spoken about the ’democratisation of the economy’ — this is our slogan.”

item.title: GolfHouse Diplomatic Cup 2026 concludes in Dhaka with diplomats, corporate leaders participating
item.description: Organisers said the event is part of a broader initiative to integrate golf into corporate culture and strengthen engagement between diplomats and business leaders through informal interaction, emphasising networking, discipline and leadership.

item.title: “Priority must go to dignified livelihood and safety for migrants abroad”
item.description: “We have to put human dignity first. Our human rights infrastructure needs to be strengthened. We’ve got to make sure our workers can earn a dignified living and stay safe while they’re abroad.”

item.title: Runa Laila to receive ‘Minar-E-Dilli’ honour
item.description: The honour will be presented to her at the 15th Delhi International Film Festival in Delhi.

item.title: Second T20I abandoned due to rain
item.description: The third and final match of the series is scheduled for May 2 at Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium, starting at 2:00 pm.

item.title: “Strong possibility of a food crisis if Hormuz issue not resolved”
item.description: “Thirty-four percent of the world’s fertilisers come through the Strait of Hormuz. Due to the blockage, there is a strong possibility of a food crisis in the coming months if this issue is not resolved.”

item.title: We can bounce back, says Shariful Islam
item.description: Pace bowler Shariful Islam, who returned figures of two for 27 from his 10 overs in the first match, insisted the side can bounce back.

item.title: Asha Bhosle, one of India’s most versatile Bollywood singers, passes away at 92
item.description: She was admitted to the hospital on late Saturday with a chest infection and exhaustion, her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle said in a social media post.

item.title: BCB board dissolved, Tamim Iqbal to lead ad hoc committee
item.description: The council informed the International Cricket Council of the dissolution by email and has submitted the ad hoc committee’s name for approval.

item.title: An extraordinary story of survival retold on screen
item.description: Each day brought uncertainty and the film brings that experience to the big screen.

item.title: “We must continue to push for regional cooperation, increase people-to-people dialogue”
item.description: “As residents of South Asia, we must continue to push for regional cooperation and increase people-to-people dialogue. This is essential for trade connectivity. For instance, the South Asian Free Trade Agreement (SAFTA) exists, but it has not been able to work effectively due to logistical and paperwork constraints.”

item.title: Bangladesh archery team accorded warm reception after Asia Cup gold
item.description: The youth and sports secretary welcomed the team with floral wreaths. He extended his heartfelt congratulations to the players, coaches, and officials for their outstanding achievement.

item.title: A major milestone for Bangladesh’s cinema industry
item.description: Competing against 11 other features in the Big Screen Competition at the International Film Festival Rotterdam (IFFR), the Bangladeshi film “Master” has earned its place among the world’s most prestigious film festivals.

item.title: Bangladeshi folk film ‘Behula Dorodi’ to screen at international festivals
item.description: Jahidul Islam, the producer, said he aims to establish Bengali cinema as a viable commercial proposition internationally while preserving “dying folk traditions” for a new generation through the film.

item.title: Warner Bros rejects Paramount’s revised offer, sets 7-day deadline for better bid
item.description: Paramount informally broached an even higher share price, $31 a share, Warner Bros said, apparently enticing the board to the table. However, its response to Paramount still makes it clear that Warner Bros prefers the Netflix deal, and the odds of a switch are long.
